target.name: EvolutionaryScale
target.founder: Alexander Rives, Tom Sercu, Salvatore Candido

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $142M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
EvolutionaryScale is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Alexander Rives, Tom Sercu, and Salvatore Candido that has raised $142M to date. The valuation is anchored within a Biotech & Health sector universe encompassing 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach. Using $142M in total raised as a proxy for base value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $355M. This method assumes capital deployed reflects early-stage value creation and that the step-up multiple reasonably captures risk reduction and milestone achievement between rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $302M to $408M, centered on a $355M median. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, and peer-median triangulation is not viable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds (only 1 narrow peer identified). Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue or operational metrics to validate the $142M raised against standard biotech value drivers, and limited visibility into how this company's capital efficiency and technology development compare to the identified private peer set.
